text: Quite a few familiar names in the LIVE program: <https://2024.splashcon.org/home/live-2024#program|https://2024.splashcon.org/home/live-2024#program>

attachments.text:
For more information, including a helpful FAQ, please visit <http://liveprog.org|liveprog.org>. 
Programming is cognitively demanding, and too difficult. LIVE is a workshop exploring new user interfaces that improve the immediacy, usability, and learnability of programming. Whereas PL research traditionally focuses on programs, LIVE focuses more on the activity of programming. 
Programmers don’t materialize programs out of thin air, but construct them out of existing programs. Embracing this insight leads to a different focus at LIVE compared to traditional PL workshops. Here are some of the qualities that we c ...

text: I'll be a remote participant, presenting at Onwards! <@U5STGTB3J> and <@U03R0B9U1GD> are presenting at Onwards! as well.

text: An OBD2 reader for your make/model + an arduino-OBD2 library helps a car owner grow closer to their hunk of metal for sure!

elements.text: An OBD2 reader for your make/model + an arduino-OBD2 library helps a car owner grow closer to their hunk of metal for sure!

text: The good news is I learned where my car's spare tire is. Not where I thought! Conviviality requires some curiosity from me as well.

elements.text: The good news is I learned where my car's spare tire is. Not where I thought! Conviviality requires some curiosity from me as well.

text: The first time I learnt that a spare in the US was a baby tire called a doughnut was years into driving there. Had a flat near Joshua Tree, opened the floorboards and thought, "whoa they gave me the tire to the wrong car!"

elements.text: The first time I learnt that a spare in the US was a baby tire called a doughnut was years into driving there. Had a flat near Joshua Tree, opened the floorboards and thought, "whoa they gave me the tire to the wrong car!"

text: I sadly won't be there, but have a joint paper with my student at HATRA (<https://2024.splashcon.org/home/hatra-2024#event-overview>) about making interactive theorem provers more interactive (somewhat in "augmenting human intellect" style...) - and would love to hear what people here think about that!

text: Hey everyone going to Splash Pasadena 2024! Does it make sense to have a separate communication channel for those of us going there in person to coordinate activities at and around the conference together? I’m thinking anything from coffees, lunches, dinners, parties, ride-shares from and to airports and hotels, etc. If such a group already exists, please let me know. Otherwise, here’s a Signal group I just created that could become that channel. Feel free to join via the <https://signal.group/#CjQKIABnPSNHVB1io9i8RS6HGgJJY8CFgbVBHh9tuK5eAxO-EhDznr08QefpowJUlzhT-guv|invite link> if you will be in LA/Pasadena, California during Splash and interested in connecting for such activities.
